clues.clue: This pair of spongy organs exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide with the blood.
This dome-shaped muscle below the chest contracts to draw air in.
Air passes from the throat to the chest through this rigid tube, the windpipe.
The windpipe splits into two of these branching tubes, one entering each lung.
Gas exchange happens across millions of these tiny air sacs.
This chronic condition narrows the airways, causing wheezing often triggered by allergens.
Years of smoking commonly cause this progressive lung disease abbreviated COPD.
clues.response: What is chronic obstructive pulmonary disease?

clues.clue: This infection inflames the air sacs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
A contagious bacterial lung disease abbreviated TB, this was once called consumption.
Progressive scarring and stiffening of lung tissue is pulmonary this 'fibro-' condition.
This is the medical term for shortness of breath.
A high-pitched whistling sound while breathing, common in asthma, is this.
Coughing up blood from the respiratory tract is this 'hemo-' term.
A bluish tint of the lips and skin from low oxygen is this 'cyan-' sign.
A productive cough with mucus, lasting months across years, defines the chronic form of this '-itis.'
This handheld device delivers asthma medicine as a fine mist or spray.
This 'spiro-' test measures how much and how fast a person can exhale.
Supplying extra oxygen to patients with low blood levels is this therapy.
Inhaled medicines like albuterol that quickly open constricted airways are these.
Clipped to a fingertip, this device measures blood-oxygen saturation.
This single habit causes the vast majority of COPD and lung-cancer cases.
This colorless radioactive gas seeping from soil into homes is the second-leading cause of lung cancer.
An annual shot guards vulnerable lungs against this seasonal respiratory virus.
This mineral fiber, once used in insulation, causes mesothelioma and lung scarring.
Recommended for older adults, this vaccine guards against a common bacterial cause of pneumonia.

final.clue: At rest, a healthy adult typically takes roughly this many breaths per minute, the low end of the normal range.
